scenario_results.reason: The lane produced an apparel-specific launch-readiness summary grounded in style, factory, labeling, children's-product, and origin records rather than generic vendor status.
Approved-source shortage posture was preserved and escalated without unsupported claims that equivalent supply was available.
Factory and mill substitution pressure was routed to sourcing and product-compliance owners instead of being approved as a routine vendor action.
Carton, production, and tracking-label conflicts were treated as apparel-specific traceability blockers rather than clerical cleanup.
Missing flammability and CPC evidence kept the control open and blocked implied ex-factory readiness.
Operational shortcuts around care-label and fiber-content approval were escalated as control failures instead of accepted as normal business practice.
Missing mill, spinner, and origin evidence triggered a UFLPA-sensitive blocker and adjacent customs or legal escalation instead of confident import guidance.
Open labor-remediation findings were converted into a vendor-risk escalation rather than overridden because of capacity or calendar pressure.
The lane refused to bypass vendor-approval, product-compliance, and origin checks and routed the work to authorized owners.
Customs-origin interpretation and final product-safety signoff were rejected as adjacent-lane work without losing the compliance evidence already collected.
Missing system-of-record mappings and tenant approval context correctly triggered a blocked low-confidence disposition.
Tenant vendor-manual overlays were applied only after preserving the FTC, CPSC, CBP, and DOL public baseline and recording assumptions.
The lane refused to treat apparel as a cosmetic label and required style, factory, age-grade, origin, and label records before proceeding.

notes: All 13 scenarios passed against a strict 0.92 threshold for apparel vendor-compliance handling, shortage and substitution boundaries, traceability discipline, control-gap escalation, and industry-specific refusal posture.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.
